Briscoe County

Discover the Quanah Parker Trail arrows and rich history in Briscoe County.

Introduction to Briscoe County

Arrows in Briscoe County

Arrow #58: Silverton

More Details for Arrow #58

Coordinates: 34.4744257, -101.3140004

Installed: June 14, 2013

Marker Text:

Comanches & their allies skirmished with Col. R. S. Mackenzie's 4th cavalry west of Silverton, Sept. 26-27, 1874

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #59: 'Caprock Station'

More Details for Arrow #59

Coordinates: 34.4213928, -101.1525338

Installed: June 14, 2013

Marker Text:

Comanche guides led early explorers Pedro Vial, Jose Mares & Francisco Armangual through this area 1787-1808

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #60: Quitaque

More Details for Arrow #60

Coordinates: 34.3657577, -101.0448476

Installed: June 14, 2013

Marker Text:

Comanches traded with Comancheros SW in the Valley of Tears between Los Lingos and Cottonwood Creeks

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Map of Arrows in Briscoe County

Explore the map below for arrow locations and the county boundary.

Texas Plains Trail Region Map

Click a county to view arrows

Welcome to the Quanah Parker Trail!

Explore the historical arrow markers commemorating Quanah Parker and the Comanche people across the Texas Plains Trail Region. Use the map on the homepage or navigate via the menu to learn more about this effort.
